source-discipline.reason: The ratification re-measures ledger state at current HEAD by the same non-destructive procedure: migrate_db.py --dry-run reports 0 pending / 187 applied and data_migrations COUNT(*) = 187, confirming step 2 already executed. No invented figures; supersedes the stale 113/66 figures measured at the older HEAD 0de84c6.

evidence-verification-gate-for-synthesis.reason: No BPC synthesis claim or citation is introduced; this DR governs the data-layer reproducibility contract only, not synthesis content.

adherence-logging-and-attestation.reason: This updated attestation plus the [DOCTRINE: 3da73bd] token on the ratification commit satisfy rule #11(a)/(b); doctrine_sha matches HEAD:governance/mission-and-epistemics.md.

bias_direction: Ratifying 3a (exempt-by-ownership) is the lowest-friction option and the one the prior analysis recommended; the bias risk is toward rubber-stamping the convenient choice -- declaring two tables exempt rather than making the source-verification job emit reproducible migrations (3b) -- because 3a requires zero engineering. Mitigation: 3a matches current reality (enrichment/log tables, not synthesis content), the exemption is named and bounded to two tables with an explicit write-contract, and it is reversible; the 7 core invariants covering all synthesis-bearing content still reproduce.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: An independent reviewer would object that 3a permanently weakens the stated reproducibility guarantee: 'the committed DB must be reproducible from migration history' silently becomes 'the 7 core invariants reproduce,' and any future scheduled job could claim the same job-owned exemption, eroding the contract by precedent. Counter: the exemption is explicit (annotated in audit.yml and recorded in the DR), bounded to two named tables with a documented authoritative writer, and the DR requires a further DR to add any new exempt table, so the contract cannot erode silently. The architecture wording is flagged for owner alignment so spec and enforcement converge rather than drift.
